channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Smoke from fire at California lithium battery plant raises concerns about air quality
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A fire at the world’s largest battery storage plant in Northern California smoldered Friday after sending plumes of toxic smoke into the atmosphere, leading to the evacuation of up to 1,500 people. The blaze also shook up the young battery storage industry.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: New York City mayor meets with Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams said his meeting with President-elect Donald Trump on Friday went “well,” but declined to immediately talk about what they discussed. The Democratic mayor flew to Florida to see the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's swearing-in will move inside the Capitol Rotunda because of intense cold weather
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will take the oath of office from inside the Capitol Rotunda on Monday due to forecasts of intense cold weather, upending months of meticulous planning for a massive outdoor event with crowds sprawling down the National Mall.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former Oakland mayor, longtime partner indicted in alleged bribery scheme
item.description: OAKLAND (AP) — Former Oakland Mayor Sheng Thao, her longtime partner and two local businessmen were named in an indictment unsealed Friday alleging the four conspired to funnel city business to companies owned by the men in exchange for money and benefits to Thao and her partner.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The baby names going 'extinct' in 2025, according to BabyCenter
item.description: Much like the doomed flightless dodo, baby names like “Emory,” “Jamie” or “Remy” are quickly being stamped out by the same humans who once found them so uniquely remarkable.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas cheerleader accused of poisoning rival's show goat barred from caring for her pets on her own
item.description: Aubrey Vanlandingham, 17, cannot care for any animals and can only visit her own pets under adult supervision after allegedly force-feeding poison to a rival student's show goat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman mistakenly declared dead after funeral home's typo
item.description: A Maryland woman's life was turned upside down after a simple typo led to her being declared legally dead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Will spring arrive early? What NOAA says
item.description: A La Niña winter just started, but it isn't expected to last long. National forecasters are already looking ahead to the spring season. A new long-range forecast released Thursday shows broad weather predictions for February, March and April nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Judges hand down what could be the last punishments for the Jan. 6 riot before pardons from Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge who oversaw Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case denounced efforts to rewrite the history of the U.S. Capitol attack Friday as she handed down what she acknowledged may be one of the final punishments for the Jan.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's inauguration will be held inside. What to know if you're traveling to Washington
item.description: The decision to move Monday's swearing-in means thousands of people with plans to visit Washington won’t be able to see President-elect Donald Trump’s second inauguration in person.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wisdom of a California grandmother, 90, goes viral amid Eaton Fire tragedy
item.description: A nearly 90-year-old California woman who lost her home to the Eaton Fire is going viral for pragmatism and positivity in the face of so much adversity.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will spring have showers? NOAA releases weather predictions for California
item.description: A La Niña winter just started, but it isn't expected to last long. National forecasters are already looking ahead to the spring season.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: FBI assistant special agent in charge who said New Orleans attack 'not a terrorist event' reassigned
item.description: FBI Assistant Special Agent in Charge Alethea Duncan has been reassigned after she told the public the New Orleans attack was "not an act of terror," sources tell Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants one of the agents who covered him after he was shot to lead the Secret Service
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will choose to lead the U.S. Secret Service one of the agents who covered him after a gunmen opened fire at the candidate during a campaign rally in Pennsylvania. Donald Trump Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump inauguration moved indoors because of freezing temperatures
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration ceremony is being moved indoors because of expected bitter cold Monday in Washington, D.C. Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance are expected to be sworn in inside the Capitol Rotunda rather than on the West Front of the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Q&A: 'God Bless the USA' singer Lee Greenwood on Donald Trump's inauguration and Bibles
item.description: Forty years ago, Lee Greenwood released “God Bless the U.S.A.,” a patriotic anthem that has become a standard at political gatherings. He's performed the song for a number of presidents, including Ronald Reagan and George H. W. Bush.
item.dc:creator: THE ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself. It’d be a first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ronald Reagan probably didn't realize he was starting a tradition when he wrote a note congratulating his successor and left it in the Oval Office desk drawer after two terms as president. He did that for George H.W. Bush, his successor and vice president of eight years.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas lawmakers urge Trump's Secretary of State pick to get tough with Mexico on water payments
item.description: Several Texas lawmakers have asked President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for Secretary to State to help urge Mexico to pay the water it owes the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Bruce Willis shakes hands with first responders in rare public appearance
item.description: Amid the devastating wildfires in Southern California, Bruce Willis took time to thank first responders.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Essential tech to have on hand for emergencies
item.description: A week ago, when a company named EcoFlow handed me a solar-powered hat that could charge my phone, I laughed. Now, I realize just how important tech like this can be in the wake of a disaster.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: FDA will allow Zyn nicotine pouches to stay on the market
item.description: Zyn products will be allowed to stay on the market after federal health officials said Thursday that the popular nicotine pouches can help adult smokers cut back or switch completely.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court upholds TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday unanimously upheld a law requiring TikTok’s China-based parent company to divest from the app, teeing up a ban set to take effect Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KTLA 5

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KRON4

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Southern California native paid off mortgage day before Eaton Fire consumed home
item.description: While those displaced by the Eaton and Palisades fires are still reeling from the loss of their homes and, in some cases, loved ones, many of them say they’re being victimized yet again by price-gouging landlords.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: California is years behind in implementing a law to make homes more fire resistant
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Reeling from destructive wildfires, including the deadliest in California history, state lawmakers in 2020 passed new requirements for clearing combustible materials like dead plants and wooden furniture within 5 feet (1.5 meters) of homes in risky areas.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top music acts announced for FireAid, a concert to support L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: The biggest names in pop, rock and country are coming together to raise funds for victims of the Palisades and Eaton wildfires and assist with the recovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Driver in Texas migrant smuggling run that led to the deaths of 53 people pleads guilty
item.description: A Texas truck driver charged in the deaths of 53 migrants who rode in a sweltering tractor-trailer with no air conditioning pleaded guilty Thursday over the 2022 tragedy that became the nation's deadliest smuggling attempt across the U.S.-Mexico border. Homero Zamorano Jr.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WEBER and LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: O'Reilly: Trump's past mob dealings prepared him to handle Putin
item.description: Bill O'Reilly thinks Trump's willingness to talk with anyone, even the mob, will serve him well when dealing with Putin.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Ash and other dangers mean LA area residents who fled fires a week ago won't be going home soon
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It has been more than a week since two massive fires forced tens of thousands of people to flee their homes in the Los Angeles area, and officials said Thursday residents won't be going home soon.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: As Biden warns of an 'oligarchy,' Trump will be flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden's pointed warning about the U.S. becoming an “ oligarchy ” of tech billionaires will be illustrated at Donald Trump's inauguration, when the world's three richest men will sit on the dais as Trump is sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Police chase suspects on bikes in Los Angeles area
item.description: Police chased two suspects in the Los Angeles area as they fled on motor bikes, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American accused of assaulting a Pennsylvania student is extradited from France to the US
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — An American accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said “So I raped you” was extradited Thursday from France to the United States. Ian Cleary, 31, of Saratoga, California, arrived in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY, SYLVIE CORBET and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Starship upper stage lost on seventh test flight, debris seen spewing in sky
item.description: Rocket debris from a SpaceX's Starship Super Heavy booster was spotted in the sky after a "rapid unscheduled disassembly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg backs Carrie Underwood inauguration performance
item.description: The comments about Underwood’s inauguration performance are notable coming from Goldberg, who is commonly a harsh critic of Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4
